rule: Tables can be created manually by defining columns and rows or automatically generated from collections of objects (POCOs).
unit_test: [Fact] public void ConsoleTable_Creation_ShouldSupportManualAndPocoMethods() { var manualTable = new ConsoleTable("Name", "Age"); manualTable.AddRow("John Doe", 30); Assert.Equal(2, manualTable.Columns.Count); Assert.Single(manualTable.Rows); Assert.Equal("Name", manualTable.Columns[0]); var pocoData = new List<SamplePoco> { new SamplePoco { Id = 1, Description = "Test POCO" } }; var autoTable = ConsoleTable.From(pocoData); Assert.Contains("Id", autoTable.Columns); Assert.Contains("Description", autoTable.Columns); Assert.Single(autoTable.Rows); } private class SamplePoco { public int Id { get; set; } public string Description { get; set; } }

rule: Table output and appearance are customizable through predefined visual styles—including Default, Alternative, and Minimal—and configuration settings such as column alignment.
unit_test: [Fact] public void TableShouldSupportDifferentFormatsAndAlignment() { var table = new ConsoleTable("Id", "Name").AddRow(1, "Test").Configure(o => o.NumberAlignment = Alignment.Right); var defaultStyle = table.ToString(); var alternativeStyle = table.ToStringAlternative(); var minimalStyle = table.ToMinimalString(); Assert.NotEqual(defaultStyle, alternativeStyle); Assert.NotEqual(defaultStyle, minimalStyle); Assert.NotEqual(alternativeStyle, minimalStyle); Assert.Equal(Alignment.Right, table.Options.NumberAlignment); }

rule: Table columns must be defined before adding rows, and the number of values in each row must match the number of defined columns.
unit_test: [Fact] public void AddRow_ShouldThrowException_WhenColumnsAreMissingOrCountMismatches() { var table = new ConsoleTable(); var exception1 = Assert.Throws<System.Exception>(() => table.AddRow("test")); Assert.Equal("Please set the columns first", exception1.Message); table.AddColumn(new[] { "Col1", "Col2" }); var exception2 = Assert.Throws<System.Exception>(() => table.AddRow("only one")); Assert.Contains("The number columns in the row (2) does not match the values (1)", exception2.Message); table.AddRow("item1", "item2"); Assert.Single(table.Rows); }

rule: Cell content exceeding the MaxWidth is automatically wrapped into sub-rows, using WordBreakDelimiters where possible to prevent mid-word splits.
Visual width and table alignment calculations must account for Unicode character widths.
unit_test: [Fact] public void ShouldMaintainTableAlignmentWhenUsingWideUnicodeCharacters() { var table = new ConsoleTable("Language", "Sample"); table.AddRow("English", "Hello"); table.AddRow("Chinese", "你好"); var output = table.ToString(); var lines = output.Split(new[] { '\n', '\r' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries); var englishRow = lines.FirstOrDefault(l => l.Contains("English")); var chineseRow = lines.FirstOrDefault(l => l.Contains("Chinese")); Assert.NotNull(englishRow); Assert.NotNull(chineseRow); Assert.Equal(englishRow.Length, chineseRow.Length); int englishValueWidth = ConsoleTable.GetTextWidth("Hello"); int chineseValueWidth = ConsoleTable.GetTextWidth("你好"); Assert.Equal(5, englishValueWidth); Assert.Equal(4, chineseValueWidth); Assert.Contains("| English  | Hello  |", output); Assert.Contains("| Chinese  | 你好   |", output); }

rule: Tables can be populated from hierarchical dictionary data structures where outer keys represent row labels.
unit_test: [Fact] public void FromDictionary_ShouldMapOuterKeysToFirstColumnAsRowLabels() { var data = new Dictionary<string, Dictionary<string, object>> { { "Row1", new Dictionary<string, object> { { "Header1", "Val1" } } }, { "Row2", new Dictionary<string, object> { { "Header1", "Val2" } } } }; var table = ConsoleTable.FromDictionary(data); var markdown = table.ToMarkDownString(); Assert.Contains("| Row1 | Val1 |", markdown); Assert.Contains("| Row2 | Val2 |", markdown); Assert.StartsWith("|   | Header1 |", markdown.Trim()); }

rule: Table output supports mixed data types and correctly handles multi-byte/Unicode character widths.
unit_test:
[Fact] public void ShouldHandleMixedDataTypesAndUnicodeWidths() { var table = new ConsoleTable("ID", "Value", "UnicodeText"); table.AddRow(1, 12.5, "Hello World"); table.AddRow(2, 100.0m, "你好世界"); table.AddRow(3, 0.99f, "Emoji: 🚀"); var output = table.ToString().Replace("
", "
"); Assert.Contains("12.5", output); Assert.Contains("100.0", output); Assert.Contains("你好世界", output); Assert.Contains("🚀", output); var lines = output.Split('
'); var unicodeLine = lines.FirstOrDefault(l => l.Contains("你好世界")); Assert.NotNull(unicodeLine); Assert.True(unicodeLine.Trim().StartsWith("|") && unicodeLine.Trim().EndsWith("|"), "Unicode row alignment failed"); }

rule: Table display and formatting options, such as numeric column alignment and row count footer visibility, are configurable.
unit_test:
[Fact] public void ShouldRespectConfigurationOptionsForAlignmentAndCountVisibility() { var data = new[] { new { Name = "Item1", Value = 100 } }; var table = ConsoleTable.From(data); table.Configure(o => { o.NumberAlignment = Alignment.Right; o.EnableCount = false; }); var result = table.ToString(); Assert.DoesNotContain("Count:", result); var expected = """ -------------------  | Name  | Value     | -------------------  | Item1 |       100 | -------------------  """.Replace("
", "
"); Assert.Equal(expected.Trim(), result.Trim().Replace("
", "
")); }

rule: Table formatting and layout support Unicode character widths, configurable cell alignment, and multiple output styles such as Markdown and standard grids.
unit_test: [Fact] public void TableShouldSupportUnicodeAlignmentAndMultipleStyles() { var rows = new[] { new { Text = "汉字Test", Value = 123 } }; var table = ConsoleTable.From(rows).Configure(o => { o.NumberAlignment = Alignment.Right; o.EnableCount = false; }); string markdown = table.ToMarkDownString(); string standard = table.ToString(); Assert.Contains("| Text     | Value |", markdown); Assert.Contains("| 汉字Test |   123 |", markdown); Assert.Contains("| 汉字Test | 123   |", standard); Assert.Contains("----------", standard); }

rule: Table output is directed to the standard system console by default.
unit_test: [Fact] public void OutputShouldDefaultToConsoleOut() { var users = new List<User> { new() { Name = "Alexandre" , Age = 36 } }; var table = ConsoleTable.From(users); Assert.Equal(table.Options.OutputTo, Console.Out); }

rule: Tables can be populated from various data sources including Lists, Dictionaries, DataTables, and Object Arrays.
unit_test: [Fact] public void TestObjectArrayTablePopulation() { object[][] data = new object[][] { new object[] { "Header1", "Header2" }, new object[] { "Value1", "Value2" }, new object[] { "Value3", "Value4" } }; var table = ConsoleTable.From(data); Assert.Equal(2, table.Columns.Count); Assert.Equal("Header1", table.Columns[0]); Assert.Equal("Header2", table.Columns[1]); Assert.Equal(2, table.Rows.Count); Assert.Equal("Value1", table.Rows[0][0]); Assert.Equal("Value4", table.Rows[1][1]); }

rule: Table structure requires columns to be defined before rows are added, and row data must match the established column count.
unit_test: [Fact] public void AddRow_ShouldEnforceColumnDefinitionAndCountMatch() { var table = new ConsoleTable(); var ex1 = Assert.Throws<Exception>(() => table.AddRow("one", "two")); Assert.Equal("Please set the columns first", ex1.Message); table.AddColumn(new[] { "Col1", "Col2" }); var ex2 = Assert.Throws<Exception>(() => table.AddRow("only one")); Assert.Contains("does not match the values", ex2.Message); var ex3 = Assert.Throws<Exception>(() => table.AddRow("one", "two", "three")); Assert.Contains("does not match the values", ex3.Message); }
